# Setup Routines

The remote kernel needs to be speified by the user. The SSH config will
typically be called by other routines since the port is usually changing
each time.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/drscotthawley/ipyfernel/blob/main/ipyfernel/core.py#L19"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### register_remote_kernel

>  register_remote_kernel (remote_python='/path/to/python',
>                              kernel_name='ipyf_remote_kernel',
>                              display_name='Remote Python',
>                              ssh_host_alias='remote_server_sshpyk',
>                              remote_kernel_name='python3', language='python',
>                              verbose=True)

*registers which python kernel will be used on remote machine*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>remote_python</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>/path/to/python</td>
<td>Full path of Python executable to run on remote system.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>kernel_name</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>ipyf_remote_kernel</td>
<td>Any old name will do. This is fine.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>display_name</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>Remote Python</td>
<td>This is just what you’ll see when you look at a list.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ssh_host_alias</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>remote_server_sshpyk</td>
<td>Same alias as was used in writing to ssh config file.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>remote_kernel_name</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>python3</td>
<td>Typical for Jupiter.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>language</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>python</td>
<td>Probably want to leave this unless you want to try R.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>verbose</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>Print extra info.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

``` python
register_remote_kernel(remote_python='/Users/shawley/exercises/solveit/.venv/bin/python')
```

    ipyf_remote_kernel is already a registered kernel

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/drscotthawley/ipyfernel/blob/main/ipyfernel/core.py#L43"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### set_ssh_config

>  set_ssh_config (port:int, user:str='', alias='remote_server_sshpyk',
>                      proxyname='bore.pub', config_path='~/.ssh/config')

*(called by ipf_startup) sets up user’s ssh .config file with info used
later*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>port</td>
<td>int</td>
<td></td>
<td>Port number on proxy server (e.g. bore.pub)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>user</td>
<td>str</td>
<td></td>
<td>Username on remote system.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>alias</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>remote_server_sshpyk</td>
<td>Default alias for <code>sshpyk</code>, leave it alone</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>proxyname</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>bore.pub</td>
<td>Have tested this with bore</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>config_path</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>~/.ssh/config</td>
<td>Shouldn’t need to change this.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

``` python
# Demo that:
# On remote machine run:  bore local 22 --to bore.pub
# Take that port and put it here
port = 3365
set_ssh_config(port)
```

    /app/data/.ssh/config file updated.

# Lower-Level Routines

These back-end routines typically don’t need to be called directly by
the user; rather they’re called by the user interface routines.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/drscotthawley/ipyfernel/blob/main/ipyfernel/core.py#L86"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### ipf_startup

>  ipf_startup (kernel_name='ipyf_remote_kernel')

*Start up the remote kernel*

``` python
ipf_startup()
```

    Success: remote kernel started

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/drscotthawley/ipyfernel/blob/main/ipyfernel/core.py#L118"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### ipf_exec

>  ipf_exec (code:str, verbose=False)

*Execute code on the remote kernel.*

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>code</td>
<td>str</td>
<td></td>
<td>Code to be executed</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>verbose</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>Return details about remote execution.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

``` python
code = """
import platform 
print(platform.system())
"""
ipf_exec(code)
```

    Darwin

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/drscotthawley/ipyfernel/blob/main/ipyfernel/core.py#L129"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### ipf_shutdown

>  ipf_shutdown (verbose=True)

*Terminates the remote kernel*

``` python
ipf_shutdown()
```

    Shutting down remote kernel

# User Interface Routines

These are the main routines you’ll typically use:

1.  Start and stop remote the remote kernel
2.  iPython magics `%%remote` and `%%local`
3.  (optional) “Sticky” mode, to redirect cell execution.

## Start and Stop Remote Kernel

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/drscotthawley/ipyfernel/blob/main/ipyfernel/core.py#L140"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### start_remote

>  start_remote (port, user='')

*Configure ssh connection to remote server and start remote server*

``` python
start_remote(port)
```

    /app/data/.ssh/config file updated.
    Success: remote kernel started

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/drscotthawley/ipyfernel/blob/main/ipyfernel/core.py#L150"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### stop_remote

>  stop_remote ()

*shutdown remote server*

## iPython Magics

These line or cell magics, invoked via ‘%’ or ‘%%’ respectively, will
direct execution of a line or cell to occur on the appropriate system –
“remote” or “local”, where local means in the current notebook.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/drscotthawley/ipyfernel/blob/main/ipyfernel/core.py#L173"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### remote

>  remote (line, cell=None)

*remote exeuction: works as %remote and as %%remote*

``` python
#%%remote    <-- docs are filtering magics but that's what's used here
import socket 
hostname = socket.gethostname()   # let's make sure we're running remotely
print("Hello from",hostname)
```

    Hello from Chonk

^ “Chonk” is the name of my home laptop

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/drscotthawley/ipyfernel/blob/main/ipyfernel/core.py#L179"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### local

>  local (line, cell=None)

*local execution: works as %local and as %%local*

``` python
#%%local <-- docs are filtering magics but that's what's used here
import socket 
hostname = socket.gethostname()   # let's make sure we're running remotely
print("Hello from",hostname)
```

    Hello from 549d6fca895f

^“549d6fca895f” happens to be the name of the solvent instance currently
running.

## ‘Sticky’/‘Seamless’ Remote Excution

via Input Transformers. These can make cells set execute remotely by
default.

**WARNINGS**: 1. Solve it is not intended to work with people modifying
input transformers So be wary. Nevertheless, this seems to work. 2. If
they’re commands that you definitely want to execute locally, maybe run
`%unset_sticky` just to be sure first.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/drscotthawley/ipyfernel/blob/main/ipyfernel/core.py#L188"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### set_sticky

>  set_sticky ()

*Makes code cells execute remotely, via input transformer*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/drscotthawley/ipyfernel/blob/main/ipyfernel/core.py#L199"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### unset_sticky

>  unset_sticky ()

*Un-sticks remote execution for code cells*

``` python
stop_remote()

print()
start_remote(port)
set_sticky()
```

    Code cells will now run locally.
    Shutting down remote kernel

    /app/data/.ssh/config file updated.
    Success: remote kernel started
    Code cells will now execute remotely.

``` python
# remote execution
import socket 
hostname = socket.gethostname()
print("Hello from",hostname)
```

    Hello from Chonk

``` python
unset_sticky()
```

    Code cells will now run locally.

``` python
# local execution
import socket 
hostname = socket.gethostname()
print("Hello from",hostname)
```

    Hello from 549d6fca895f
